title: Custom Business Gift Cards
description: How can I obtain customizable physical gift cards for my business?
narrative: The user wants to purchase bulk physical gift cards that can be branded with a business name, offered in various denominations, and recognized by point‑of‑sale or loyalty software. Relevant documents describe ready‑made packs (e.g., 100 or 500 cards), theme options (Christmas, Mother’s Day, etc.), barcode integration, and personalization features. Documents about digital gift cards, unrelated services, or mobile‑app reward schemes do not satisfy this need.

title: Lamb Tagine Discussion
description: What informal comments, experiences, or casual tips do people share about cooking or eating lamb tagine?
narrative: The user is looking for user‑generated, conversational content that mentions lamb tagine – anecdotes, personal stories, casual advice, or social media chatter about the dish. Relevant documents are informal posts that reference lamb tagine in a personal context. Documents that consist of formal recipes, ingredient lists, step‑by‑step cooking instructions, or purely commercial food content are not relevant.

title: Lotus Car Sales
description: What used Lotus cars are currently available for purchase in Carignan, Quebec?
narrative: The user wants to locate advertisements or inventory listings of Lotus automobiles being sold in the Carignan region, including details such as price, model year, mileage, condition, and seller type. Relevant documents should present Lotus car listings specific to Carignan (or nearby locales) and provide searchable filters or contact information. Documents that discuss unrelated Lotus parts, other car brands, general automotive news, or unrelated forums are not relevant.

title: Veal Paupiettes
description: What is a traditional recipe and cooking method for paupiettes de veau?
narrative: The user is looking for a complete French veal paupiette recipe, including a list of ingredients, stuffing suggestions, step‑by‑step preparation, cooking technique (e.g., braising, roasting), cooking times, and serving ideas. Relevant documents should provide detailed culinary instructions for preparing paupiettes de veau, focusing on the cooking process rather than unrelated farm product listings or non‑culinary content.

title: Car Sun Protection
description: What products and methods can be used to protect a vehicle’s interior and occupants from solar heat and UV radiation?
narrative: The user is seeking information on ways to shield a car from the sun, including tinted window films, solar films, and other UV‑blocking solutions that reduce heat, glare, and interior fading while complying with local regulations. Relevant documents should discuss film specifications (e.g., UV‑blocking percentages, visible light transmission), installation processes, legal limits for front‑window tint, and effectiveness for passenger comfort. Documents about unrelated topics such as hotels, baby sunscreen, or general visor accessories are not relevant.

title: Small Vessel Records
description: What are notable examples of the smallest boats that have successfully completed long ocean voyages?
narrative: The user is interested in information about very small seaworthy vessels—typically under 6 feet in length—that have undertaken significant crossings such as trans‑Atlantic or other open‑sea voyages. Relevant documents should describe specific boats, their builders, dimensions, the routes taken, the challenges faced, and any records or achievements associated with these miniature crafts. Materials that focus on generic ship types, large vessels, or unrelated topics (e.g., naval modelling forums, medical treatments) are not relevant.

title: Passenger Cars
description: What are the characteristics, options, and market opportunities related to passenger cars?
narrative: The user is seeking information about passenger cars, including their specifications, available models, usage scenarios, and commercial opportunities. Relevant documents should describe passenger car features, mileage, year of vehicle, or business offerings that target passenger car buyers or operators. Documents focusing on vans, rental services, vehicle taxes, licensing, or unrelated vehicle types are not relevant.

title: War Education
description: How does armed conflict affect schooling and what arrangements exist for children displaced by war?
narrative: The user is interested in information about the relationship between war and schools, including how wars disrupt education, how host countries organize schooling for refugee or displaced children, and the broader debate around compulsory education in conflict contexts. Relevant documents describe Ukrainian refugees attending French schools, special programs for allophone pupils, and discussions of compulsory schooling. Documents that merely mention the word "school" without any connection to war, conflict, or displaced learners are not relevant.

title: Option Right
description: How does the right of option work at Pôle Emploi, and what are the eligibility criteria and steps to request it?
narrative: The user wants a full description of the "right of option" (droit d'option) offered by Pôle Emploi. Relevant information includes the definition of the right, the conditions that must be met (e.g., unexhausted previous benefits, allowance thresholds, minimum periods of work, percentage increase requirements), the administrative procedure for submitting a written request, the cooling‑off period, and the impact on existing benefits (loss of lower rights, changes in duration and amount). The narrative should also cover the pros and cons of exercising the option and provide illustrative examples. Documents that discuss these aspects are relevant, whereas texts that only mention unrelated regulations, training accounts, internal delegations, or other Pôle Emploi administrative matters are not relevant.

title: AXA Client Portal
description: How do I access, log in to, and use the AXA client space for managing my insurance policies?
narrative: The user is looking for information about AXA's online client space (customer portal). Relevant documents should explain what the client space is, how to sign up or log in, how to recover a forgotten username or password, how to navigate the portal to view or modify policies, submit claims, or terminate contracts. Documents that merely discuss AXA internal agreements, unrelated corporate information, or generic insurance industry trends are not relevant.

title: Top Electric Cars
description: What are the best electric cars currently available?
narrative: The user is looking for comparative information that identifies the highest‑rated electric passenger vehicles on the market, including rankings, key specifications (range, performance, price), and consumer or expert reviews that justify why those models are considered the best. Relevant documents should directly discuss or list specific electric car models, evaluate their strengths and weaknesses, and provide data useful for making a purchase decision. Documents that only mention electric‑car related imagery, generic discussions of vehicle leasing, policy, or unrelated car‑sharing services without concrete evaluation of electric car models are not relevant.

title: Potato Late Blight
description: What are effective methods for identifying, preventing, and managing late blight (Phytophthora infestans) in potatoes?
narrative: The user wants detailed guidance on late blight affecting potatoes, covering disease symptoms, conditions that promote infection, scouting, chemical and cultural control options, resistant varieties, and storage issues. Relevant material explains the pathogen, its life cycle, symptom description, environmental risk factors, fungicide and translaminar treatments, cultural practices (crop rotation, soil pH, planting depth, ridging), and lists resistant cultivars. Content limited to product listings, unrelated garden magazines, or non‑potato topics is not relevant.

title: Heaven to Earth
description: Find resources that let me view the planet from a heavenly (satellite) perspective and then transition to detailed, ground‑level (street) imagery.
narrative: The user wants a way to experience Earth as seen from above—like a view from heaven—and then drill down to the dusky, mundane surface. Relevant documents should describe tools that provide both global aerial/satellite images and the ability to zoom in to street‑level panoramas (e.g., Google Earth, Google Maps), including features such as 3D models, historical image timelines, and navigation between sky‑view and ground‑view. References to the spiritual notion of a "new heaven and new earth" are optional but may be included if they relate to the metaphor of moving from a celestial to an earthly viewpoint. Only sources that explain how to view Earth from space and then explore it on the ground meet the relevance criteria.

title: Mad Space History
description: What are the most bizarre, controversial, or chaotic events in the history of space exploration?
narrative: The user is seeking information about the “mad” side of space history – unusual, dramatic, or scandalous episodes that stand out from the ordinary technical narrative. Relevant documents should describe strange or spectacular incidents such as early animal flights (e.g., Laïka), failed or wrecked spacecraft, eccentric missions, conspiracy‑type claims, or other sensational stories that illustrate a chaotic or “mad” aspect of humanity’s venture into space. Materials that merely provide general space science, routine mission overviews, or unrelated historical topics (e.g., British history, hatters, arts festivals) are not relevant.

title: Legacy Office
description: What is the Legacy Office, what does it do, and what projects or activities is it associated with?
narrative: The user is looking for information about an entity named “Legacy Office” (or a similarly‑named office such as a production office). Relevant material should identify the organization, its business purpose (e.g., media production, corporate holding), its location, and any notable projects it has carried out (such as the video production for Serre Chevalier’s Bike Park). Documents that describe a production company called “Mountains Legacy Production” or corporate filings that reference a legacy‑related office are considered relevant, while unrelated technical manuals, security upgrade guides, or unrelated historical articles are not.

title: Prevail Meaning
description: What is the definition of “to prevail” and how is it used in English?
narrative: The user is looking for a clear explanation of the verb “to prevail,” including its definition, synonyms (e.g., win, overcome, surpass), typical contexts, and example sentences. Relevant results should describe the meaning of prevail, show it as a synonym of “win” or “surpass,” and provide usage in statements like “peace and humanity must prevail.” Documents that only mention unrelated topics (e.g., sports scores, forum chatter) are not relevant.

title: Pioneering Ventures
description: What are notable historical examples of people or technologies that ventured into previously unexplored or risky domains?
narrative: The user is looking for accounts of bold, groundbreaking actions that can be described as “entering uncharted territory.” Relevant materials should describe such pioneering moves, whether in the political‑cultural sphere (e.g., Jean Moulin’s symbolic entrance into the Pantheon as a landmark moment of resistance) or in engineering/transport (e.g., the Renault Colorale pickup designed for difficult terrain). Documents that narrate these pioneering events are judged relevant, while unrelated topics such as menopause stories, video‑game trailers, or commercial advertisements are not.

title: Mashed Potato Recipe
description: What is a simple recipe for preparing crushed (mashed) potatoes?
narrative: The user is looking for a cooking guide that explains how to make crushed or mashed potatoes, including ingredient lists, preparation steps, and serving suggestions. Relevant documents are those that provide a potato‑mashed recipe with instructions, tips, and variations. Documents about mechanical crushers, mining equipment, poetry, or unrelated topics are not relevant.

title: Citizen Portal
description: What is the "Citizen Space" (online portal) and what services does it provide for residents?
narrative: The user is looking for information about municipal or governmental online portals often called "Citizen Space" or "Espace Citoyen". Relevant documents describe these portals, how to log in, the types of e‑services offered (e.g., administrative formalities, vaccine pass, personal account, mobile app), and links to the portal itself. Documents that discuss unrelated physical spaces such as hotel rooms, conference venues, or retail locations are not relevant.

title: Energy Supplier Info
description: What energy suppliers operate in a given area and what services do they provide?
narrative: The user is looking for information about companies that supply electricity, gas, or renewable energy to end‑users, including their names, regional coverage, types of contracts, and any partnership with distribution networks. Relevant documents should mention specific suppliers, their offerings, and how they interact with the local distribution system. Documents that only discuss switching procedures, regulatory FAQs, or generic market liberalisation without naming or describing particular suppliers are not relevant.

title: Kia Car Overview
description: What general information is available about Kia automobiles, including brand history, model lineup, and dealer services?
narrative: The user is looking for a broad knowledge base on Kia as a car manufacturer rather than specific listings of used vehicles or aftermarket parts. Relevant documents should provide an overview of Kia’s corporate history, current model range (e.g., Niro, Stonic, Rio), promotional offers, and details about dealership services such as sales and after‑sales support. Documents that focus solely on individual used‑car advertisements, unrelated vehicle parts, or technical part numbers are not relevant.

title: Gallic Symbol
description: What is the meaning and cultural significance of the term “The Gallic” in France?
narrative: The user is looking for information about the phrase “The Gallic” as it is used to denote a French national emblem or symbol. Relevant documents should describe the Gallic rooster (le coq gaulois), its history, symbolism, appearances on coins, flags, official badges, sporting logos, and monuments. Documents that focus on unrelated topics such as Asterix comics, Roman‑Gaul wars, or modern media unrelated to the rooster symbol are not relevant.

title: Faro Car Rental
description: What car rental agencies, rates, and booking details are available in Faro, Portugal?
narrative: The user is seeking information about renting a vehicle in the city of Faro. Relevant documents should provide details on car‑rental providers operating in Faro, pricing, reservation requirements, pick‑up locations (e.g., airport or city centre), vehicle types, and any special services or policies specific to Faro. Documents that discuss car rentals in other locations, unrelated services, or general travel information without a focus on Faro are not relevant.

title: Nantes Car Rental
description: Looking for information on car rental services in Nantes, France.
narrative: The user is seeking options to rent a vehicle in the city of Nantes (the query contains a typo "nants"). Relevant documents are those that describe car rental providers, offers, locations, pricing, or vehicle types specifically for Nantes, such as listings of standard or luxury rentals in the area. Documents that discuss unrelated travel services, accommodation, or locations other than Nantes are not relevant.

title: Potato Diseases
description: What diseases affect potatoes and what are their impacts on the crop and human health?
narrative: The user seeks information about diseases that affect potatoes, including plant pathogens such as late blight (Phytophthora infestans) and historical outbreaks, as well as health concerns related to toxic compounds in potatoes (e.g., glycoalkaloids in green or sprouted tubers). Relevant documents discuss the biology, spread, and consequences of potato diseases for agriculture and for human consumption. Documents that focus on general farming practices, unrelated recipes, or non‑potato topics are not relevant.

title: Beaujolais Wine Tourism
description: What are the notable Beaujolais vineyards, their wines, and visitor information for tasting tours?
narrative: The user wants detailed information about Beaujolais wine producers, including estate names, wine varieties, tasting notes, prices, visiting hours, and sustainability practices, as well as cultural media that highlights the region. Relevant documents describe specific Beaujolais estates, their wines, tasting experiences, and regional film; unrelated promotional posters or generic event listings are not relevant.

title: Boat Shoes
description: What footwear is appropriate for wearing on a boat, focusing on non‑slip, water‑resistant or deck‑style shoes?
narrative: The user is seeking shoes that can be safely used on a boat. Relevant documents should describe footwear designed for boating—e.g., boat shoes, deck shoes, water‑proof or non‑slip shoes—covering features like slip‑resistant soles, quick‑dry materials, and suitability for wet decks. Documents that only discuss general boots, fashion sneakers, or unrelated boat/antique listings are not relevant.

title: Renault Space
description: What are the specifications, features, and price of the Renault Space (Renault Traffic) vehicle?
narrative: The user is seeking detailed information about Renault's "Space" model, specifically the Renault Traffic Space class van, including its specifications, available configurations, price (e.g., from €23,750 excluding VAT), and intended uses (work, leisure, family). Relevant documents should describe the Renault Traffic Space version, its marketing description, and pricing details. Documents about unrelated car models, mechanical issues, or non‑automotive topics are not relevant.

title: Corfu Car Rental
description: Where can I rent a car in Corfu and what are the prices, agencies, and booking options?
narrative: The user wants practical information for hiring a vehicle on the island of Corfu, including which rental companies operate there, pickup locations (airport, city center, etc.), typical price ranges for different car categories, availability, reservation tips, and any relevant policies (e.g., mileage limits, cancellation). Documents are relevant only if they discuss Car Rental in Corfu, providing details on agencies, costs, vehicle types, and booking advice.

title: Meeting Car Rental
description: Where can I rent a car for an upcoming business meeting?
narrative: The user wants to find a car rental service that can supply a vehicle convenient for attending a meeting. Relevant content should describe car‑rental booking interfaces, availability of vehicles, pick‑up and drop‑off locations (especially near meeting venues), pricing, and options useful for business travel such as corporate rates or flexible return policies. Documents that only list used cars for sale or unrelated travel information are not relevant.

title: Lincoln Automobiles
description: What information is available about Lincoln-branded cars, such as model line‑ups, specifications, pricing, and where to buy them?
narrative: The user is interested in the Lincoln automobile brand. Relevant documents should describe Lincoln as a vehicle manufacturer, list current and past Lincoln models, provide technical specifications, performance data, pricing, reviews, buyer guides, dealership locations, and related automotive news. Content that only references Abraham Lincoln, a financial advisory firm named Lincoln International, or any unrelated historical or corporate material is not relevant.

title: Infant Accident Guidance
description: What steps should I take to ensure my infant’s safety and health after a car accident?
narrative: The user is looking for post‑crash advice specifically concerning a baby who was in a car accident, including symptoms to monitor, when to seek medical care, and whether the car seat must be replaced. Relevant sources discuss infant injury monitoring, car‑seat replacement policies, and related insurance considerations. Content about unrelated vehicle rentals or motorcycle crashes does not meet the user’s need.

title: Bordeaux Concerts
description: Which concerts are taking place in Bordeaux and what are the details (artist, date, venue, tickets)?
narrative: The user is looking for information about live music events scheduled in Bordeaux, France. Relevant documents should mention a concert occurring in Bordeaux, providing specifics such as the performing artist(s), the date of the event, the venue (e.g., Arkea Arena), and any ticketing information. General transportation or unrelated entertainment listings that do not reference a specific concert in Bordeaux are not relevant.

title: Indre‑Loire News
description: What recent public notices, reports, or announcements mention both Indre‑et‑Loire and the Loire (department or river)?
narrative: The user is looking for documents that contain information about both Indre‑et‑Loire and the Loire region (or the Loire river). Relevant items are official or public‑facing materials such as regional reports, financial summaries, death notices, or other announcements that reference both places. Documents that only mention one of the locations, or that are unrelated personal ads or dating content, are not relevant.

title: Energy Mediation
description: What entities or processes act as mediators for conflicts and negotiations in the energy sector?
narrative: The user seeks information on the role of mediators—individuals, organizations, or technological systems—that facilitate resolution of disputes, policy negotiations, or implementation of energy‑related projects. Relevant documents should describe mediation activities (e.g., political mediation missions) and/or energy‑focused initiatives that serve a mediating function such as energy‑efficiency programs, management systems, or facilitation bodies that bridge stakeholder interests in the energy domain. Non‑relevant material includes unrelated legal‑practice listings or generic professional directories.

title: Office Transfer
description: What are the legal and fiscal procedures for transferring a public or ministerial office in France?
narrative: The user is seeking authoritative information on the administration of public or ministerial offices, specifically how such offices can be assigned or transferred, the mandatory registration formalities, applicable taxes and fees, the role of the right of presentation, and the conditions that must be satisfied for governmental approval of a successor. Relevant documents should describe the relevant articles of the French Finance Act, the General Tax Code provisions, the registration process, fee calculations, and any special tariff regimes or suspensive conditions. Documents that focus on unrelated topics such as job listings, office rentals, or computer hardware are not relevant.

title: Silver Photography
description: How can I make, develop, and print silver (black‑and‑white) photographs?
narrative: The user is looking for information about the traditional silver‑based photographic process, including how to develop negatives, print silver images, available formats, and where to access a silver photo lab or workshops. Relevant documents describe silver photography labs, equipment (enlargers, chemicals, trays), format specifications (3:2 silver formats), and learning resources. Non‑relevant documents are those about unrelated products such as jewelry, custom prints, or general e‑commerce sites.

title: Leek Quiche Recipe
description: How can I make a leek quiche, optionally with variations such as a crustless, vegetarian, or goat‑cheese version?
narrative: The user is seeking a complete recipe for a quiche that features leeks as the primary ingredient. Relevant results should provide ingredient lists, preparation steps, cooking times, and may discuss optional variations (e.g., using goat cheese, making it crustless, gluten‑free, or adding turmeric). Content that merely mentions leeks or quiche in passing, or unrelated topics, is not relevant.

title: Free Car Games
description: Where can I find free online car games that can be played in a web browser?
narrative: The user wants to locate web‑based, no‑download, free car‑themed games, preferably suitable for children or casual players. Relevant documents should describe collections or portals that offer such browser games (e.g., racing, rally, truck, or car simulator games) and note that they are free to play. Documents focusing on specific car models, downloadable APKs, unrelated mobile apps, or non‑gaming car services are not relevant.

title: Employer Mobility Aid
description: What programs or benefits allow employers to provide mobility assistance or subsidies for employee commuting?
narrative: The user seeks information about employment‑related mobility support, specifically employer‑funded or employer‑offered mobility aids and subsidies that help workers commute. Relevant content includes descriptions of the Sustainable Mobility Package, employer budget allocations (e.g., up to €500‑€600 per employee per year), eligible soft‑mobility services (bicycles, scooters, car‑pooling, public transport, shared‑mobility vehicles), legal frameworks, implementation guidelines, and management options for such schemes. Documents that focus solely on general job‑search tools, disability‑specific mobility devices, or regional transport feasibility studies without reference to employer‑provided mobility benefits are not relevant.

title: SFR Space
description: What information exists about SFR products or services that are related to space, or about space news that mentions SFR?
narrative: Relevant documents are those that either describe SFR (the French telecommunications brand) offerings such as USB chargers, accessories, or other products, or discuss space‑related topics such as launches, missions, or space news. A document is considered relevant if it contains the term “SFR” in the context of a product/service, or if it contains the term “space” (or a space‑related event) regardless of whether SFR is mentioned. Documents that only contain unrelated fiction, unrelated brand names, or marketing content that does not reference SFR or space are not relevant.

title: Oil Lamp Safety
description: What are the best safety practices for operating a modern oil lamp?
narrative: The user wants practical guidance on using an oil lamp safely, covering topics such as suitable oil types, wick preparation, filling limits, placement, child‑proofing, fire‑prevention, and general handling tips. Relevant documents are those that provide clear, actionable safety instructions or warnings for oil lamps. Documents that discuss unrelated subjects—such as auction listings, pressure‑lamp technical details, historical descriptions without safety advice, or decorative aspects—are not relevant.

title: Madeira Car Rental
description: What car rental options, prices, and booking details are available for traveling in Madeira?
narrative: The user is looking for practical information on renting a vehicle on the island of Madeira (also spelled Madere), including which companies operate there, pickup/drop‑off locations (especially the airport), vehicle types, rates, insurance coverage, and any special conditions. Relevant documents must explicitly discuss car rental services in Madeira and provide details useful for planning a rental; documents that only mention flights, unrelated destinations, or car rentals in other locations are not relevant.

title: New Potatoes
description: What defines a “new potato”, how is it used in cooking, and which recently bred varieties are marketed as new potatoes?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive overview of new potatoes: the botanical and culinary definition (young, early‑harvest tubers often sold with skins on), historical French expressions such as “potatoes in robes of the fields”, typical seasonality and cooking characteristics, and examples of modern early‑maturing cultivars (e.g., the Gourmandine variety derived from Charlotte × Estima, its harvest timing, yield and texture). Documents covering these points are relevant; sources about potato disease control, generic recipes, or unrelated topics are not.

title: Return to Work Bonus
description: What financial incentives or bonuses are available for employees who return to the workplace after teleworking?
narrative: The user is looking for official programs, employer policies, or government schemes that provide a monetary bonus, tax credit, or other financial incentive to workers who resume on‑site work after a period of remote work. Relevant documents should describe the nature of such bonuses (e.g., employment bonus, PEP), eligibility criteria, amount or calculation method, and any related tax or legal implications. Documents that discuss general return‑to‑work procedures, safety measures, or telework termination without mentioning a bonus are marginally relevant only if they also reference a financial incentive tied to the return. Content unrelated to employment incentives (e.g., casino or gaming bonuses) is not relevant.
